text: they’re married . “Not that it means all that much to everyone. Heard about a lot of people who broke their vows…” She frowned down at the ring. “Maybe you’re right. I don’t know why we still bother .” Ronin reached out, plucking the ring from her palm. He raised it to eye-level, turning and tilting it, examining even the inside of the band. Taking her hand in his, he slowly slipped the ring onto her finger . Lara’s breath hitched, eyes widening, and her whole body tensed . “You still bother because it means…hope. That everything will be okay, some day. Because it means a chance at a future in a world that wants to destroy us. It means you are not alone.” He covered her hand with both of his, meeting her gaze and holding it. “Let this ring be my vow. To protect you. To provide for you. To give you all you need that’s within my power, and find a way to give you whatever’s not .” The air fled her lungs. “Did you…did you just kind of… marry me ?” “I’ve given you my vow .” She searched his face. This was one of those times when she couldn’t read him, couldn’t tell how to take this. After what she’d just told him, how could this be anything but him becoming her husband? The idea of being married to Ronin wasn’t as disagreeable as she might have thought. He was a man, regardless of what was beneath his skin, and he’d treated her better than anyone apart from Tabitha . “Then I vow to protect you, too, to the best of my ability. To place my trust in you and never break yours. To remind you, every day, that you are alive .” “I gave my word freely, Lara. I don’t ask anything in return.” His voice was soft, confusion on his features . “I gave mine, too.” She leaned forward, brushing her lips against his. “Unless…you don’t accept ?” “Part of me says I shouldn’t .” “Oh.” Lara drew back, a sinking feeling in her stomach. Maybe he didn’t really understand . He held her hand, preventing her from pulling away. “I don’t want to put you in unnecessary danger. That’s been my existence, Lara…one danger after another. But I haven’t been so damaged in the Dust that I could ever reject you. I accept.” Dipping his head, he raised her hands to his lips, kissing her knuckles. There was the hint of a smile on his face when he looked up at her. “Did you just kind of marry me ?” Something fluttered inside Lara. She grinned. “I don’t have a ring to give you, but yeah. I guess I did .” * * * L ara sat at the window sill, using a small brush to wipe the dirt and grime off her ring. Even hours later, she still couldn’t quite believe it; they were married . Perhaps that didn’t mean much to people anymore. Perhaps it was some outdated leftover from a dead world. That didn’t matter, because it meant something to her. It meant something to them both . Dropping the brush into the nearby bowl of water, she lifted a corner of her shirt and dabbed the moisture from the ring. When she lifted it into the late afternoon sunlight, her breath caught in her throat . The gold band gleamed, at once darker and brighter than she thought it could be. The metal’s sheen rippled as she turned it between her fingers. But that was nothing compared to the stone atop it. Ronin had called it a diamond . It sparkled as though giving off its own light, drinking in the rays of the sun and shattering them to cast a little rainbow on her skin. It was unlike anything she’d ever seen. It was beautiful . And it was hers . If such objects were valuable in the old world, this one certainly represented months of earnings, though she couldn’t quite understand why . The bots needed gold because it was used for some of their inside parts. That they considered it valuable made sense . Why use so much of it for simple…decoration? She supposed it was a statement, a display of wealth, but what did that matter compared to food or warm clothing ? The sound of flowing water in the bathroom altered subtly as Ronin moved in the shower. There were three rooms with toilets in this house — three! — though only two had tubs. Why would anyone need so many places to relieve themselves? Was that another flaunting of wealth ? She slipped the ring back onto her finger. It was loose, but not enough so to slip off. It was strange seeing it on her hand — a hand that had regularly clawed through dirt and debris, a hand that was only just losing its callouses. When she first found the ring, her only thoughts had been of the credit it would fetch. Of the food it would purchase . Now…she was married . Even if it was just between her and Ronin, without a… representative of authority , or whatever, it was no less real. He wanted to keep her safe, to feed her, to make her happy. The ring had become a symbol of all that . They’d come together three more times after exchanging their vows, each time somehow more intense than the last, despite her growing weariness. Lara’s body was sated, for now, though her hunger for him was by no means diminished . There’d been no talk of love. Ronin had displayed many emotions since she met him, but could he really love someone? Could she love him? It wasn’t necessary, for a marriage, and he was already more than she could have ever dreamed for — he was kind, patient, and caring, always placing her needs before his own .
